title: n8n workflow created but not activated
symptom: Webhook or schedule trigger does not fire. Workflow exists but does not respond to incoming events.
applies_when: Workflow uses Webhook, Schedule, or event-based trigger nodes
Workflow was recently created or updated
root_cause: n8n workflows must be explicitly activated (toggled ON) before they respond to triggers. A newly created or recently edited workflow defaults to inactive state. The webhook URL is only registered when the workflow is active.
fix_steps: Open the workflow in the n8n editor
Toggle the 'Active' switch in the top-right corner to ON
Verify the webhook URL is registered by checking the Webhook node's URL display
Test by sending a request to the webhook URL
agent_instruction: Activate the n8n workflow by toggling the Active switch ON in the top-right corner of the editor. Webhook URLs are only registered when the workflow is active.
